name: Ketu
description: Ketu, also known as the South Node of the Moon, is a shadow planet in Hindu astrology. He is considered a malefic influence, representing karmic influences, spirituality, liberation, and detachment. Ketu is often depicted as a headless body, symbolizing the cutting off of worldly desires and attachments. He is associated with hidden enemies, psychic abilities, and sudden, unexpected events. Ketu's influence can bring about profound spiritual insights and a deep understanding of the self, but it can also manifest as confusion, isolation, and a lack of material success. He represents the past life karma that needs to be addressed in the present. While often feared, Ketu is ultimately considered a force for spiritual growth and liberation, pushing individuals towards self-realization by detaching them from worldly illusions. Ketu's placement in a birth chart is believed to indicate areas where an individual has already mastered skills in past lives and may experience a sense of detachment or lack of interest in this lifetime. He is a powerful force that can lead to enlightenment through hardship and self-reflection. He is one of the Navagrahas (nine planets) in Hindu astrology.
subtitle: South Node: Detachment, Spirituality, Liberation

keyMyths.title: Samudra Manthana
keyMyths.description: During the churning of the ocean, Ketu, along with Rahu, attempted to drink the Amrita (nectar of immortality). Vishnu, in the form of Mohini, decapitated them, leaving them as separate entities.

keyMyths.title: Ketu's Nature
keyMyths.description: Ketu is considered a headless body, symbolizing the cutting off of worldly attachments and desires. This detachment allows for spiritual growth and liberation from the cycle of rebirth.

keyMyths.title: Ketu and Past Life Karma
keyMyths.description: Ketu represents past life karma that needs to be addressed in the present life. His influence often brings challenges and hardships that lead to spiritual awakening and self-realization.

sources.text: A comprehensive guide to Vedic astrology.

worshipPractices.name: Ketu Graha Shanti Puja
worshipPractices.description: A ritual performed to appease Ketu and mitigate its malefic effects. It involves chanting mantras, offering prayers, and performing specific rituals to reduce negative karmic influences and promote spiritual growth.

worshipPractices.name: Fasting on Tuesdays
worshipPractices.description: Devotees sometimes observe fasts on Tuesdays to honor Ketu. This practice is believed to reduce the negative impact of Ketu in one's astrological chart and to gain spiritual merit.

festivals.name: No specific festival dedicated solely to Ketu
festivals.description: While there is no dedicated festival for Ketu, he is worshipped during Navagraha Pujas (worship of the nine planets) and other auspicious occasions. His influence is always considered during important life events and astrological consultations.

cultural.modernLegacy: In modern Hindu astrology, Ketu remains a significant planet influencing personal horoscopes and astrological predictions. He is consulted for understanding karmic debts, spiritual inclinations, and potential life challenges. Remedies, such as gemstone recommendations and mantra chanting, are often prescribed to mitigate negative effects attributed to Ketu.

extendedContent.title: Origins & Birth
extendedContent.content: In Hindu mythology, Ketu's origin is intertwined with the Samudra Manthan, the churning of the cosmic ocean. During this event, the gods and demons churned the ocean to obtain Amrita, the nectar of immortality. When the Amrita emerged, Vishnu, in his Mohini avatar, distributed it only to the gods. A demon named Rahu, disguised as a god, attempted to drink the Amrita. However, Surya (the Sun) and Chandra (the Moon) recognized him and alerted Mohini. Before the Amrita could pass his throat, Mohini severed Rahu's head with her Sudarshana Chakra. The head became Rahu, and the body became Ketu. Thus, Ketu is depicted as a headless body, symbolizing detachment and the severing of ties with the material world. This origin story highlights Ketu's association with illusion, deception, and the consequences of past actions.

extendedContent.title: Major Myths & Stories
extendedContent.content: While Ketu doesn't have as many independent stories as other deities, his role in the tale of Samudra Manthan is pivotal. He is forever linked with Rahu, representing the opposing forces of illusion and liberation. Ketu's headless form symbolizes the cutting off of ego and worldly attachments, leading to spiritual insight. Another significant aspect is Ketu's association with sudden and unexpected events. He is believed to bring about sudden losses or gains, often related to past karmas. Stories often depict individuals experiencing drastic shifts in fortune due to Ketu's influence, highlighting the unpredictable nature of life and the importance of spiritual detachment. These narratives reinforce Ketu's role as a karmic force, pushing individuals towards self-realization and liberation from the cycle of birth and death.

extendedContent.title: Worship & Cult
extendedContent.content: Ketu is primarily worshipped through astrological remedies and rituals aimed at mitigating its malefic effects. Devotees often perform Ketu Graha Shanti Pujas, which involve chanting specific mantras dedicated to Ketu, offering prayers, and performing other symbolic rituals. These practices are believed to pacify Ketu and reduce its negative influence on an individual's life. Gemstones associated with Ketu, such as cat's eye, are sometimes recommended to strengthen positive influences or counteract negative ones. Fasting on Tuesdays is also observed by some devotees as a means of appeasing Ketu and gaining spiritual merit. While there are no specific temples dedicated solely to Ketu, he is worshipped as part of the Navagrahas in many Hindu temples, where devotees offer prayers and seek blessings for protection from negative karmic influences.

extendedContent.title: Legacy & Influence
extendedContent.content: Ketu's legacy in Hindu astrology is profound, shaping the understanding of karma, spirituality, and detachment. He represents the karmic baggage carried from past lives and its influence on the present. Ketu encourages introspection, self-realization, and liberation from worldly desires. His influence is seen in individuals drawn to spiritual pursuits, those with psychic abilities, and those who experience sudden, unexpected life events. In modern times, Ketu continues to be a significant factor in astrological readings, influencing decisions related to career, relationships, and spiritual growth. Remedies and practices aimed at mitigating Ketu's negative effects remain popular, highlighting the enduring impact of this shadow planet on Hindu beliefs and practices. He serves as a reminder of the importance of embracing change, seeking spiritual enlightenment, and understanding the consequences of our actions.

shortDescription: Ketu, the South Node of the Moon in Hindu astrology, is a shadow planet influencing karma, spirituality, and detachment. Often depicted headless, symbolizing liberation.
